Il s’agit d’un programme de chansons traditionnelles de France et du Québec, dont le vocabulaire est axé sur les activités estivales : les vacances, l’eau, le soleil, les animaux et les couleurs. Le programme comprend des chansons à répondre, des chansons accompagnées d’instruments de musique et des jeux musicaux. C’est une expérience interactive qui encourage la participation active des enfants. Aucune inscrip

events.title: A Very Gru-Some Summer Movie Series

In the sequel to 2016's Zootopia , newly-promoted detectives Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde investigate a mysterious pit viper named Gary De'Snake. Defying police orders, the duo goes on the run through new city sectors, uncovering a massive conspiracy involving the displaced reptile population and the city's corrupt founding families.

events.title: Family Movie: Hoppers
events.description: Join us this Summer at the library for a screening of Hoppers Movie. Animal lover Mabel seizes an opportunity to use a new technology to 'hop' her consciousness into a lifelike robotic beaver and communicate directly with animals. As she makes amazing discoveries, Mabel befriends a charismatic beaver and must rally the entire animal kingdom to face a major, imminent, human threat Rated G. 1hr 45mins. English Subtitles for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (SDH) when available.

events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.  Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. Please submit your request at least 2 weeks in advance to: tpl.ca/accessibility-events.

events.title: Story Planet Comic Lab
events.description: The Artists in the Library series comes to Evelyn Gregory Library this Fall!  Led by professional illustrators Hilary Leung and Joseph Bonsu, children will explore the art of comics and build a portfolio of work culminating in a polished long-form comic. The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Pokémon Club
events.description: Whether you're a new or expert Pokémon Trainer, everyone is welcome! Show off your Pokémon knowledge and research skills and join our exciting Pokémon scavenger hunt for free Pokémon cards! Feel free to bring in your old Pokémon cards to play with or trade with staff and other Pokémon trainers!

events.title: Japanese Friendship Bracelet Making for Kids!
events.description: Join us as we make kumihimo friendship bracelets! Kumihimo translates literally to "gathered threads" and is a traditional Japanese braiding technique and artform with a rich history. Ages 6+. Drop-in. Large Groups and camps must call ahead to attend.

events.description: Join us to observe the Sun safely using telescopes fitted with certified solar filters. On a clear day, participants will explore sunspots, prominences, and other solar features in real time. A short presentation on the Sun will be given at the beginning. This program will be run by two PhD students studying astronomy at the University of Toronto. They'll be happy to answer your 'burning' questions about the sun and space. For all ages! events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Saturday Movie: The Girl Who Cried Pearls and Higglety Piggley
events.description: Due to Hillcrest Library closure on July 27, this event will be postponed until January 2027. In Montreal, at the dawn of the 20th century, a poor boy falls in love with a girl whose sorrow turns into pearls. He sells them to a ruthless pawnbroker, who hungers for more. Tempted by greed, the boy must choose between love and fortune. The choice could damn his soul.
